title: AST Officer Involved Shooting Investigation: Bethel [AK26026271]
summary: At the request of the Bethel Police Department, the Alaska Bureau of Investigation is investigating an officer-involved shooting that occurred in Bethel on March 25, 2026, at approximately 4:05 am. A preliminary investigation indicates an adult male had been taken to the Bethel hospital earlier in the night after standing in the roadway and refusing to move onto the sidewalk. The man left the hospital before being seen and later returned to the roadway. Officers contacted the man a second time, at which point he produced a knife and refused multiple commands to drop it. The man then approached an officer while brandishing the knife, and the officer fired, striking him. The man was medevaced to an Anchorage-area hospital for treatment of serious injuries. No other injuries were reported to
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: the request of the Bethel Police Department

title: AST Vehicle Pursuit: Wasilla [AK26026211]
summary: On 03/24/2026 at approximately 2255 hours, the Alaska State Troopers conducted a traffic stop on a black 2002 Mercedes-Benz C230 near Knik-Goose Bay Road and Eagle Eye Circle for speeding and swerving in its lane. The vehicle failed to stop, leading Troopers on a 20-mile pursuit with speeds reaching 125mph. The vehicle crashed at the end of Knik-Goose Bay Road near the Goose Bay airstrip and the driver fled on foot into the woods. Troopers located the driver, identified as Trinton Spohnholz, age 21 of Anchorage, hiding under a fallen tree and arrested him without further incident. Trinton was remanded at MSPT for Failure to Stop 1 and Reckless Driving and held with no bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Residential Fire Fatality: Fairbanks [AK26025408]
summary: On March 22, 2026, at 1:38pm, University Fire Department received a report of a residential trailer fire in Goldrush Estates in Fairbanks. On arrival crews found heavy fire involvement throughout the trailer. Information was received from neighbors that the structure was likely occupied at the time of the fire. During a primary search of the structure, fire crews found and extracted a male adult with significant burn injuries. He was transported to a local area hospital where he succumbed to his burn injuries. Department of Public Safety Deputy Fire Marshals from Fairbanks responded to conduct and origin and cause investigation. The body is being transported to the State Medical Examiner's Office for confirmation of identification and autopsy. No foul play is suspected at this time.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: arrival crews found heavy fire involvement throughout the trailer

Chappel also had two outstanding warrants and was remanded to Spring Creek Correctional Center and held without bail.

title: AST SAR / Missing Person: Aleknagik [AK26024529]
summary: Update: On March 21, 2026, at 8:30am, the Alaska State Troopers in Dillingham were notified by Dillingham Police Department that Jonathan was located and was transported back home to Aleknagik. No injuries were reported. Investigation is still ongoing regarding the missing snowmachine. Jonathan has been removed from missing person status. On March 20, 2026, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a missing person in Aleknagik. Jonathan Wassillie, age 34 of Aleknagik, was last seen leaving the Aleknagik church on March 15, 2026, on foot. It is believed that he may have left town on a snowmachine, which was also reported missing that same day. title: AST Fugitive from Justice: North Pole [AK26024720]
summary: On March 20, 2026, the Alaska State Troopers assisted the Sandusky County Ohio Sheriff's Office. The Alaska State Troopers contacted James Bergman, 50YOA, and arrested on his fully extraditable warrant out of Ohio for failing to appear for sentencing for the crimes of Importuning, Attempted unlawful sexual conduct with minor, compelling prostitution, and disseminating matter harmful to juveniles. James was remanded to the Fairbanks Correctional Center.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST DV / Arson: Stebbins [AK26024115]
summary: On March 18, 2026, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a residential fire in Stebbins. Investigation revealed the fire stemmed from a domestic violence disturbance inside the home. During the incident, 43-year-old Connie Acoman intentionally set the walls of the residence on fire. At the time, two adults and two small children were inside the home and were placed in danger. After starting the fire, Acoman fled onto the Bering Sea, sea ice, where she was later detained by Stebbins VPOs. AST from Nome responded to Stebbins and took custody of Acoman. She was remanded to AMCC without further incident.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: revealed the fire stemmed from a domestic violence disturbance inside the home

title: AST DV/ASSAULT: Fairbanks [AK26024655]
summary: On 03/20/2026, the Alaska State Troopers received a report of a domestic disturbance in the Fairbanks area. Dispatch advised that Amelia Paul was on the phone yelling and stating that her boyfriend was beating her up. Upon arrival at the residence, both parties were contacted and separately interviewed. Investigation revealed that Amelia was the primary physical aggressor and had assaulted the victim. Amelia was placed under arrest and transported to Fairbanks Correctional center where she checked on by the nurse then remanded.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST VCOR/DWLR: Delta Junction [AK26024640]
summary: On March 20, 2026, at approximately 2245 hours, AST conducted a traffic stop in Delta Junction for an equipment violation. The driver was identified as Sierra Mahnke, age 35. Records check revealed that Mahnke was on conditions of realease and had a revoked license with multiple previous convictions. Mahnke was arrested for Violating Conditions of Release and Driving While License Revoked. She was transported to FCC on those charges and held on bail.

title: AST Warrant Arrest: Tok [AK26015365]
summary: On March 19, 2026 the Alaska State Trooper Fairbanks Criminal Suppression Unit and Tok Patrol contacted Donny Pitka during a traffic stop at mile 1322 of the Alaska Highway. Pitka had a felony arrest warrant for Assault III, Assault IV, and Reckless Endangerment. Pitka was placed under arrest. A subsequent search warrant on Pitka's duffle bag revealed he was in possession of two semi-automatic pistols, one of which was stolen. Pitka was remanded to the Fairbanks Correctional Center on his warrant as well as two counts of misconduct involving a weapon III, for being a felon in possession of two concealable handguns. *Release has been updated with additional information.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST MICS 3, MIW 2: North Pole [AK25047292]
summary: UPDATE: After additional follow-up, Clay Kristiansen is being charged with MICS 3 and MIW 2. On 05/17/2025 at 2250 hours a Trooper conducted a traffic stop for a turn signal violation. The Trooper developed reasonable suspicion that the occupants of the vehicle were involved in narcotic activity. K9 Kenny was requested and responded to the scene. K9 Kenny had a positive indication on the vehicle. Ultimately, a search warrant was conducted and 56.5 grams of suspected methamphetamine, a scale, and a stolen handgun was seized from the vehicle. This investigation is ongoing.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
